UI / UX Audit  ·  2026-05-04

UI/UX AUDIT REPORT: Precious Metal — Account-Creation, Cross-Currency Sell & Acknowledgement Variants

A senior UX audit across nine usability dimensions with a regulated-banking lens. Findings, severity, and prioritised recommendations.
Audit Date
2026-05-04
Platform
Web Desktop (OCBC Velocity — Business Banking, ≥1024 px)
Persona
Senior UX Auditor & Design Systems Expert (NN/g lens)
Flow Scope
Figma node `693:15517` — 14 screens captured: Overview (empty + populated), Buy form (new-account variant), Buy form (existing-account variant), Sell form (insufficient holdings + cross-currency), Buy/Sell review (3 variants), Acknowledgement (3 variants including new-account-opened), Email confirmation (Buy + Sell).
4.0
UX Health Score / 10

Executive Summary

The Figma frame names in this section (`Day 1 - Add/delete users`, `Renew landing - uploaded file`) are template/working titles that do **not** describe the actual content. The 14 captured frames are in fact a **variant set of the Precious Metals Buy/Sell trade flow** — with genuinely new content for (a) opening a Precious Metals account on a customer's first buy, (b) selling XAU against a USD credit account (cross-currency), (c) an insufficient-holdings warning on Sell, and (d) an acknowledgement variant that confirms both the trade and the new account in a single screen. The audit reports what is in the pixels. The dominant problem cluster is **build readiness**: literal CMS placeholder tokens (`[Precious Metal Account]`, `[paper gold]`, `[Esolutions Alpha Pte Ltd]`) and email merge tokens (``, ``, etc) appear unresolved in production-feeling screens — and on the new-account-opened acknowledgement, the entity name is `Esolutions Beta Pte Ltd` while the session header is `Esolutions Alpha Pte Ltd`. Any one of these would erode trust on a regulated investment commit; all three together cannot ship. The **second cluster is pricing transparency**. Cross-currency Sell (XAU → USD) is shown with no FX rate, no markup, and no timing — the customer commits to a USD figure with no idea how the FX leg is priced. The on-screen "Indicative" price is never reconciled with an "Executed" price on the acknowledgement, so the user never sees what they actually paid. Terms and Conditions are referenced in the commit disclaimer but not hyperlinked. The **third cluster is workflow integrity**. The global header carries a `Tasks and Statuses` pill with a red-dot indicator on every screen — strongly implying a maker-checker queue — but no pending-approval, awaiting-checker, or rejected-by-checker state is designed anywhere. The acknowledgement copy says "filled successfully", treating the commit as terminal, which would mislead novices in any flow that actually requires a second-eye approval. The Recent orders table has no Status column. **Per-persona scores:** - **Novice (corporate customer):** **4.0 / 10** — opaque pricing, buried risk, unresolved placeholders, and "filled successfully" copy on a possibly-not-yet-final order will mislead and frustrate. - **Experienced (corporate customer):** **4.5 / 10** — flow is fast and clean operationally, but the cross-currency FX gap, missing T&C link, missing executed-price disclosure, and absent status/audit-trail surfaces will be flagged by treasury/compliance. - **Combined:** **4.0 / 10** — adequate scaffolding, critical build and disclosure gaps that must be closed before launch. **Overall UX Health Score: 4.0 / 10** — *do not ship until the four critical build-readiness findings (placeholders, merge tokens, entity mismatch) and the four critical pricing/regulatory findings (FX rate, executed price, T&C link, risk disclosure) are resolved.*

6
Critical
10
High
6

All Findings (22)

#Screen / ComponentDimensionSeverityFindingRecommendation
#1 Buy Form (new-account variant) 9. Trust & Emotional Design CRITICAL Unresolved CMS placeholder syntax leaks into UI copy. The Buy form's Credit account block reads literally 'New [Precious Metal Account] will be created with your order'; the Review screen info banner reads 'A new [Precious Metals account] will be opened with this order. This account will hold your [paper gold] … under [Esolutions Alpha Pte Ltd]'. Resolve every `[…]` token to its final string before launch. Add a lint step or pre-commit check that fails the build if any visible UI string still contains a bracketed placeholder pattern.
#2 Email — Buy confirmation 9. Trust & Emotional Design CRITICAL Email templates ship with unresolved merge tokens visible in the design: ``, ``, ``, ``, ``. If any merge step fails in production the customer will receive these tokens verbatim. Personalise all six merge tokens at template-fill time; add a fail-closed check (do not send if any `<…>` token remains unresolved). Render a fallback like 'Dear Customer' if a personal name is unavailable.
#3 Buy Acknowledgement — account-opened variant 9. Trust & Emotional Design CRITICAL Entity-name mismatch on a regulated confirmation screen. The session header reads `ESOLUTIONS ALPHA PTE LTD` but the 'Your Precious Metals account has been opened' panel attributes the new account to `Esolutions Beta Pte Ltd`. Bind every entity reference on the acknowledgement to the session's customer-ID at render time. Add a contract test that asserts entity-name consistency between header context and any per-screen entity label.
#4 Sell Form (cross-currency XAU → USD) Banking-domain — Pricing transparency CRITICAL The Sell form supports a cross-currency settlement (XAU debit account → USD credit account) but shows no FX rate, no FX timing, no spread or markup disclosure. The user commits to a USD figure with no idea how the FX leg is priced. Add a dedicated FX panel to Review whenever debit and credit currencies differ: 'XAU/SGD reference price, SGD/USD rate (with markup), all-in unit price, total USD'. Lock the FX quote with a countdown if appropriate.
#5 Buy Review Banking-domain — Pricing transparency CRITICAL The form, Review, and Acknowledgement all reference 'Indicative' figures (Indicative price, Indicative market value, Investment amount derived from an indicative price). The order completes successfully, but the executed fill price is never explicitly stated anywhere — the acknowledgement repeats the indicative amount as if it were the fill. On the acknowledgement screen and confirmation email, surface 'Executed unit price', 'Quantity', and 'Total paid' as the canonical record. Mark the on-chart figure 'Live indicative' to disambiguate.
#6 Buy Review Banking-domain — Regulatory CRITICAL Review disclaimer says 'you confirm you have read, understood and agree to the Terms and Conditions' but the words 'Terms and Conditions' are not hyperlinked. The user has no in-flow access to the document they are agreeing to. Hyperlink 'Terms and Conditions' to the actual PDF/HTML doc. Track timestamp of the link-view per user. Also add a Key Fact Sheet link separately from the T&Cs.
#7 Buy Review — new-account variant 8. Cognitive Load & Clarity HIGH Account-creation-on-first-buy messaging is split across three places with three different phrasings: form ('New [Precious Metal Account] will be created with your order'), review banner ('A new [Precious Metals account] will be opened with this order'), acknowledgement panel ('Your Precious Metals account has been opened'). Inconsistent terminology for the same event. Pick one canonical phrase ('A Precious Metals account will be opened') and use it in all three locations. Lift the message out of the Credit-account block on the form into a top-of-form info panel so it is impossible to miss.
#8 Buy Form (new-account variant) 1. Information Architecture & Navigation HIGH On the new-account-on-first-buy branch, the fact that completing this form will open a brand-new precious-metals account is communicated only inside the Credit-account block in plain text ('New [Precious Metal Account] will be created with your order'). The novice user can easily overlook that they are doing two things at once. Surface 'You will open a new Precious Metals account with this order' as a top-of-form info panel with an icon. Repeat on Review (already partially done) and Acknowledgement (already done).
#9 Sell Form (insufficient holdings) Banking-domain — Regulatory HIGH No risk disclosure or product-suitability statement surfaces anywhere in the captured flow. The disclaimer copy uses 'without recourse to Bank X' (legalese) without a plain-language counterbalance. Empty-state copy on the landing markets the product ('hedge against inflation, store of value') with no equivalent downside framing. Add a persistent risk panel above the chart on Buy/Sell forms: 'Precious metal prices can fall as well as rise. Past performance is not indicative of future results. Holdings are unallocated paper bullion and are not SDIC-insured.' Plus a Key Fact Sheet link.
#10 Overview (empty) 8. Cognitive Load & Clarity HIGH 'Indicative' is used in at least four different ways without an inline definition: 'Indicative price (SGD/ounce)' on the chart, 'Indicative market value' in Holdings details, 'Market value (Indicative)' on the Sell form, 'Quantity to buy (Indicative)' on one Buy variant. Each carries a slightly different operational meaning. Add an inline info-icon next to every 'Indicative' label opening a tooltip: 'For reference only. The price you transact at is set at execution and may differ.' Settle on one canonical noun phrase per concept.
#11 Buy Acknowledgement (expanded) 7. Feedback & System Status HIGH Acknowledgement copy reads 'Buy order for Gold (XAU) has been filled successfully' as a terminal state. Yet the global header has a 'Tasks and Statuses' pill with a red-dot indicator that typically signals pending second-eye approval in corporate banking. If a maker-checker step exists invisibly, novice users will believe the trade is final when it may not be. If maker-checker applies, change ack copy to 'Order submitted for approval. Awaiting Checker.' and only show 'Filled successfully' once both legs are completed. If single-control, explicitly state 'No further approval required — order is final.'
#12 Overview (empty) 1. Information Architecture & Navigation HIGH Every in-app screen carries a 'Tasks and Statuses' utility pill in the global header — with a red-dot indicator suggesting pending items — but the overlay/page that opens from this pill is never shown in any captured frame. For a regulated corp-banking context where this pill likely hosts the maker-checker queue, that is critically under-signposted. (a) Tooltip on hover explaining 'Tasks awaiting your approval'. (b) Open-pill drop-down designed and audited. (c) Surface pending counts in-context on the order/acknowledgement screens, not only in the global header.
#13 Overview (populated) 7. Feedback & System Status HIGH Recent orders table has no Status column. Rows show Metal / Order type / Quantity / Indicative price / Total / Date — but no Filled / Pending / Cancelled / Rejected / Awaiting-approval indicator. Add a STATUS column with coloured pills (Filled / Pending Approval / Awaiting Settlement / Cancelled / Rejected); make filterable.
#14 Buy Acknowledgement (expanded) 7. Feedback & System Status HIGH Acknowledgement gives no 'what happens next' copy beyond a single forward-looking line ('Future Precious Metals trades will settle into this account'). There is no settlement-cutoff time, no statement-availability time, no dispute-resolution path, no statement that an email has been dispatched, and no 'who else was notified'. Add a short 'What happens next' panel on the ack: '(1) Email sent to . (2) Settlement at end-of-day, SGT. (3) Statement available T+1. (4) Dispute path: …'
#15 Buy Form (new-account variant) 6. Forms & Data Entry HIGH The Sell form has a well-designed amber inline warning when the requested quantity exceeds available holdings ('Reduce the quantity to 25.86 ounces or less to proceed'). The Buy form has no equivalent insufficient-funds warning for the Debit account — the user can compose an over-budget order with no live feedback. Mirror the Sell's insufficient-holdings warning on the Buy form for insufficient-balance: 'Investment amount exceeds available balance (X SGD). Reduce the amount to proceed.' Use the same amber styling.
#16 Overview (empty) 1. Information Architecture & Navigation HIGH The FAQ accordion containing 'Things to look out for', 'Fees and charges', 'Reason to invest' sits at the bottom of the landing page, below the fold. By the time a user clicks 'Trade Gold (XAU)' or 'Start trading', they have not seen fees or risks. Surface fee headline ('Fees from X% of trade value') and risk headline ('Capital at risk — paper bullion is not SDIC-insured') *above* the per-metal trade CTAs. Keep the full FAQ below.
#17 Overview (empty) 5. Component & Interaction Design MEDIUM The Overview (empty) primary call-to-action 'Start trading' is rendered as a blue text link, while every other in-flow primary CTA is a slate filled button ('Review buy order', 'Confirm buy order', 'View order'). The most important action on the empty state has the weakest affordance. Replace the link with the standard slate primary button styled to match the rest of the product.
#18 Buy Review Banking-domain — Authorization MEDIUM The Confirm Buy/Sell order button is the only confirmation gate. There is no 2FA / SCA / hardware-token / soft-token / signing step between Review and order placement. For a real-money corporate trade, single-click confirmation is below industry norm. Insert a 2FA / SCA / soft-token prompt between Review and Confirm. If maker-checker is in use, this remains required for the maker; the checker performs their own auth at their own step.
#19 Buy Acknowledgement (collapsed) 5. Component & Interaction Design MEDIUM On the collapsed ack, the expand/collapse affordance is a bare chevron with no text label. There is no 'Show details' or 'Hide details' wording. Add a text label alongside the chevron: 'Show details ▾' / 'Hide details ▴'. Keep the chevron for visual cue.
#20 Sell Review 8. Cognitive Load & Clarity MEDIUM Buy summary band reads 'Investment amount · Quantity to buy'; Sell summary band reads 'Quantity to sell · Market value'. The primary number flips position between Buy and Sell, and the SGD-amount field changes name from 'Investment amount' to 'Market value' even though it represents the cash leg in both cases. Use a single label per direction — 'Cost (SGD)' for Buy, 'Proceeds (SGD)' for Sell — and place it in the same position in both summary bands.
#21 Overview (populated) 3. Typography & Readability MEDIUM Numeric figures in the Holdings details and Recent orders tables use a proportional sans (not tabular figures). Digits across rows do not line up vertically — decimals drift, making column scanning slower than necessary for a finance product. Apply `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums` to every numeric column in the Holdings, Recent orders, and acknowledgement summaries. Right-align numerics.
#22 Buy Form (new-account variant) design system inconsistency MEDIUM The primary navigation label is inconsistent across the design: most screens read 'FX and Investment'; the Buy/Sell trade form variants render the same nav item as 'FX and Treasury'. Same global nav, two labels for the same item. Pick one — recommend 'FX and Investment' since that's used on the customer-facing surfaces — and unify across every screen.

Annotated Screenshots

Each screenshot is annotated with numbered markers. Severity-coloured connector lines link each marker on the screenshot to its finding card on the right.

Findings on this screen:

Findings on this screen:

→ Add a dedicated FX panel to Review whenever debit and credit currencies differ: 'XAU/SGD reference price, SGD/USD rate (with markup), all-in unit price, total USD'. Lock the FX quote with a countdown if appropriate.

The disclaimer copy uses 'without recourse to Bank X' (legalese) without a plain-language counterbalance. Empty-state copy on the landing markets the product ('hedge against inflation, store of value') with no equivalent downside framing.
→ Add a persistent risk panel above the chart on Buy/Sell forms: 'Precious metal prices can fall as well as rise. Past performance is not indicative of future results. Holdings are unallocated paper bullion and are not SDIC-insured.' Plus a Key Fact Sheet link.

Sell Review

Findings on this screen:

#20 MEDIUM Buy summary band reads 'Investment amount · Quantity to buy'; Sell summary band reads 'Quantity to sell · Market value'. The primary number flips position between Buy and Sell, and the SGD-amount field changes name from 'Investment amount' to 'Market value' even though it represents the cash leg in both cases.
→ Use a single label per direction — 'Cost (SGD)' for Buy, 'Proceeds (SGD)' for Sell — and place it in the same position in both summary bands.

Top 5 Priority Recommendations

Block launch on the three "build-readiness" defects

What to fix: Three production-feeling screens show unresolved tokens — `[Precious Metal Account]` / `[paper gold]` / `[Esolutions Alpha Pte Ltd]` on the Buy form and Review banner (Finding #1); `` / `` / `` / `` / `` in both email templates (#2); and an entity-name mismatch on the account-opened acknowledgement (`Esolutions Beta Pte Ltd` shown when the session is `Esolutions Alpha Pte Ltd`) (#3).
Why it matters: Any single one of these would erode customer trust on a regulated investment commit. For a banking product the credibility cost is non-recoverable in real time — a customer who sees "Dear ` `" treats the email as phishing and may never re-open the product.
How to fix it: (a) Add a pre-launch lint that scans every rendered UI string and email template for `\[[^\]]+\]` and `<[^>]+>` patterns and fails the build if any are found in visible copy. (b) Bind every entity reference on the acknowledgement to the session's customer-ID. (c) Add a contract test that asserts entity-name consistency between header context and any per-screen entity label.
Effort estimate: **Quick Win** — instrumentation and binding only.

Make cross-currency pricing transparent

What to fix: The Sell form's cross-currency variant (XAU → USD credit account) shows no FX rate, no markup, no FX timing (#4). The form, Review, and Acknowledgement all use "Indicative" figures but never disclose the executed unit price (#5). Terms and Conditions are referenced in the commit disclaimer but not hyperlinked (#6).
Why it matters: Cross-currency settlement is two pricing decisions disguised as one. Hiding the FX leg is a regulatory and credibility failure simultaneously. The "Indicative → fill" gap is the same root issue as the live/executed price ambiguity flagged in the prior Buy/Sell audit.
How to fix it: On Review whenever debit and credit currencies differ, surface a 4-line FX panel: `XAU/SGD reference price | SGD/USD rate (with markup) | All-in unit price | Total USD`. On the Acknowledgement, replace the indicative figure with `Executed unit price · Quantity · Total paid`. Hyperlink T&Cs on Review with a view-timestamp tracker; add a Key Fact Sheet link.
Effort estimate: **Medium Lift** — Review template extension + pricing service publishes the executed unit price.

Surface risk, fees, and disclosure at decision time

What to fix: Risk content lives below the fold in a collapsed FAQ ("Things to look out for") (#16). Disclaimer copy uses "without recourse to Bank X" without a plain-language counterbalance (#9). No KFS, no "past performance" boilerplate near any chart, no custody (allocated/unallocated) statement.
Why it matters: Regulated investment requires risk visibility *before* commitment. Today's empty-state markets upside; the only downside language is buried legalese.
How to fix it: Add a persistent risk panel above the chart on Buy/Sell forms with three lines covering price volatility, past-performance disclaimer, and custody/SDIC status. Add a fee headline + risk headline above the per-metal trade CTAs on the Overview. Add a KFS link.
Effort estimate: **Medium Lift** — new component, no new logic.

Reconcile the maker-checker workflow with the acknowledgement copy

What to fix: Every screen carries a "Tasks and Statuses" pill with a red-dot, implying a maker-checker queue, but no pending-approval / awaiting-checker / rejected state is designed anywhere. The acknowledgement says "filled successfully" — terminal language (#11, #12, #13).
Why it matters: For a regulated corporate trading product, the maker-checker pattern is the operational floor. If it exists invisibly the novice user will misinterpret "filled successfully" as final. If it doesn't exist, treasury/compliance will refuse the product.
How to fix it: (a) Decide the policy: maker-checker or single-control. (b) If maker-checker, change ack copy to "Order submitted for approval. Awaiting Checker." Only show "Filled successfully" once both legs complete. Surface a STATUS column on Recent orders with `Filled / Pending Approval / Awaiting Settlement / Cancelled / Rejected`. (c) Design and audit the `Tasks and Statuses` overlay. (d) If single-control, explicitly state on the ack: "No further approval required — order is final."
Effort estimate: **Large Effort** — new screens and workflow integration.

Tighten the account-creation-on-first-buy branch

What to fix: The fact that completing this form will open a brand-new Precious Metals account is communicated only inside the Credit-account block as plain text (#8). Across three screens the messaging is written three different ways (#7).
Why it matters: Opening an account is a separate decision from placing a trade — it carries its own statement, tax treatment, and reporting implications. A novice corporate customer must not blunder into it.
How to fix it: Pick one canonical phrase ("A Precious Metals account will be opened") and use it identically on form, Review, and Acknowledgement. Lift it out of the Credit-account block into a top-of-form info panel with an icon. Add a tickbox or inline acknowledgement: "I understand a new account will be opened for this purpose."
Effort estimate: **Quick Win** — copy unification + one new info panel.

---

Accessibility Summary

WCAG CriterionLevelStatusDetails

Overall Accessibility Risk Level: HIGH

What's Working Well

Insufficient-holdings warning on Sell is well-designed.

Amber tint, clear icon, specific message ("Reduce the quantity to 25.86 ounces or less to proceed"), CTA disabled until resolved. This is the right pattern; mirror it for Buy insufficient-funds.

Account-opened acknowledgement panel concept is good

— combining the trade success with the new-account success into one screen avoids a multi-page "wizard" feel. The entity-binding bug aside, the design intent is correct.

Acknowledgement collapse pattern reduces cognitive load

for repeat users who don't need to re-read order details. (Chevron labelling aside.)

Email subject lines are clear and action-specific

("Your Gold (XAU) buy/sell order is confirmed").

Cross-currency Sell support exists in the design at all

— many bank products skip this. The architectural decision is right; only the disclosure layer needs to catch up. ---
